name: Soneja - La Dehesa Trail (PR-CV 320)
name_original: Running por el Parque Natural la Dehesa de Soneja (PR-CV 320)
description: Jog the Soneja - La Dehesa Trail (PR-CV 320), a difficult 17.0-mile route through Sierra de Espadán, featuring the unique Balsa de la Dehesa
region_intro: Jogging routes around Soneja traverse a diverse landscape in the Alto Palancia region of Castellón, Spain. The area is characterized by its natural features, including the protected Paraje Natural Municipal la Dehesa de Soneja, which spans over 680 hectares. Runners can explore cork oak and pine forests, mountain passes, and the unique Laguna de la Dehesa, the only mountain lagoon of its kind in the Valencian Community. The trails often feature "good track" surfaces, offering varied terrain for running. Best jogging routes around Soneja The most popular jogging route is Puntal de l'Aljub and Pico Bellota Loop, a 11.2 miles (18.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 55 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Soneja - La Dehesa Trail (PR-CV 320), a difficult 17.0 miles (27.4 km) path. This route leads through the heart of the protected La Dehesa natural area, showcasing its ecological value. Local runners also love the Rustic stone bridge – Hermitage of Saint Anthony of Padua loop from Sot de Ferrer, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through cultural and natural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Jogging around Soneja is defined by protected natural areas, cork oak and pine forests, and mountain passes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging ascents. The routes in Soneja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 4000 runners have used komoot to explore Soneja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Soneja?
A: Soneja offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 200 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to different preferences, ranging from easy paths to more challenging ascents through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Soneja's jogging trails?
A: The jogging trails in Soneja feature diverse terrain, often described as having a 'good track' or being 'wide trails'. You'll encounter cork oak and pine forests, mountain passes, and areas around the unique Laguna de la Dehesa. Some routes may include paved or concreted sections, while others offer gravel roads and natural paths, providing varied surfaces for your run.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Soneja?
A: Yes, Soneja offers options for all fitness levels. While many routes are challenging, there are at least 6 easy and 59 moderate routes available. A good moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32033063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rustic stone bridge – Hermitage of Saint Anthony of Padua loop from Sot de Ferrer&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.1 miles (11.4 km) long and takes about 1 hour 20 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running in Soneja?
A: Soneja's running routes often pass through the protected &lt;strong&gt;Paraje Natural Municipal la Dehesa de Soneja&lt;/strong&gt;. Key natural features include the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Dehesa&lt;/a&gt;, the only mountain lagoon of its kind in the Valencian Community, and various cork oak and pine forests. You might also encounter viewpoints offering panoramic vistas, or even historical sites like the ruins of Soneja's 19th-century Carlist Walls near the River Palancia.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in Soneja?
A: Yes, many of Soneja's running paths are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880566835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puntal de l'Aljub and Pico Bellota Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 11.2 miles (18.1 km) with significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Soneja?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, the natural areas around Soneja, including the Paraje Natural Municipal la Dehesa, are generally welcoming to dogs. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas with wildlife, and to clean up after them. Many local runners enjoy these trails with their canine companions.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in Soneja?
A: For families, especially those with younger children or looking for a less strenuous activity, the easier and moderate routes are ideal. These often feature wider, well-maintained paths suitable for a leisurely jog or walk. Look for trails that avoid steep inclines and cover shorter distances to ensure an enjoyable experience for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Soneja?
A: Soneja offers good conditions for jogging throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are advisable. Winters are typically mild, making it possible to run year-round, though some higher elevation paths might be cooler.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique geological formations to see on the running routes?
A: Yes, the region around Soneja is known for its natural beauty. While jogging, you might find yourself near highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1310997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bride's Leap Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2099448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tío Juan Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features unique geological formations and a rich hydraulic heritage, including ancient aqueducts like El Arco, which can add cultural interest to your run.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Soneja?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Soneja, particularly near trailheads or within the town itself. For routes accessing the Paraje Natural Municipal la Dehesa, designated parking areas are often provided. It's advisable to check specific route details for the most convenient parking spots.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Soneja?
A: The running routes in Soneja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 15 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the natural parks, and the variety of terrain that caters to different skill levels. The opportunity to explore cork oak forests, mountain passes, and unique natural features like the Laguna de la Dehesa is frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners in Soneja?
A: Absolutely. Soneja is home to many challenging routes, with over 150 trails classified as difficult. These often feature significant elevation gains and varied terrain. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e880555164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soneja - La Dehesa Trail (PR-CV 320)&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 17.0 miles (27.4 km) path that leads through the heart of the protected La Dehesa natural area.
tips: Beautiful views towards the Serra d'Espada Natural Park. The viewpoint is only a few meters away from the main path and you will have to be careful because the last few meters are very rocky.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Soneja - La Dehesa Trail (PR-CV 320)?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for this trail, which is approximately 27.4 kilometers long, typically takes around 3 hours and 24 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. Keep in mind that individual times may vary based on fitness levels and stops along the way.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the PR-CV 320 trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter sections of asphalt near the start, but it quickly transitions to predominantly forest tracks and rural roads. Be prepared for zigzag paths on steep slopes and areas with many loose stones and abundant ruts, especially on descents. This makes careful foot placement essential.

faqs.question: What are the main natural highlights and viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable features. A major highlight is La Balsa de la Dehesa, the only natural mountain lagoon in the Valencian Community, known for its rich riverbank vegetation. You can also enjoy panoramic views stretching from La Calderona to Espadán from rocky areas known as Los Peñascos, and visit the Mirador de la Penya Cagà.

faqs.question: Is the Soneja - La Dehesa Trail (PR-CV 320) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as 'difficult' for jogging due to its considerable climbs, descents, and challenging terrain with loose stones and ruts. It's more suited for experienced joggers and trail runners rather than beginners or casual walkers looking for an easy stroll.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for the PR-CV 320?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins near the Soneja Interpretation Center. This is a good starting point for accessing the route and usually offers parking facilities. You can also obtain more information about the natural park there.

faqs.question: Is the Soneja - La Dehesa Trail (PR-CV 320) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through a natural park, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed in the provided information. It's always best to check with the Soneja Interpretation Center or local authorities for current rules on pets, especially leash requirements, to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone and protect the local wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through a wooded environment, offering shade. However, given the challenging terrain and significant elevation changes, spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for jogging. Summers can be very hot, and some sections might be slippery after heavy rain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access the Parque Natural Municipal La Dehesa de Soneja?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not mention any specific permits or entrance fees required for jogging the PR-CV 320 trail within the Parque Natural Municipal La Dehesa de Soneja. However, it's always a good practice to confirm with the Soneja Interpretation Center for any updated regulations or special requirements.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is rich in biodiversity. You'll pass through extensive pine forests, olive groves, and cultivated fields. The area around La Balsa de la Dehesa features exuberant riverbank vegetation, including cork oaks and strawberry trees. Informative panels along the route provide details about the local flora. Wildlife includes protected species like the ocellated lizard and common hedgehog, and wild boars are also present.

faqs.question: Does the Soneja - La Dehesa Trail (PR-CV 320) intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the PR-CV 320 makes up the vast majority of this route, it does briefly intersect with other paths. You might find sections that overlap with the Camino del Pozo de Almaroz, Camino de la Fuente de Bolós, and Senda de la Moratilla.

faqs.question: Are there any historical elements to discover along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers glimpses into the region's past. You can find remnants of human activity, such as an old barite mine (Mina de Barita Canasta), ancient corrals, and an old quarry that was used for millstones, adding a historical dimension to the natural landscape.
